prevalence.trend: stable; improved diagnosis and trial access in specialist centers

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low (rare) but high per-patient disability and cardiomyopathy burden

vulnerablePopulations: patients with diabetes or scoliosis complications
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Progressive ataxia with neurologic signs plus genetic confirmation of FXN expansion; cardiology evaluation (ECG/echo) and metabolic screening

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis via neurology referral and genetic testing
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Limited (no cure); supportive multidisciplinary care improves function and reduces complications

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Neurogenetics and multidisciplinary ataxia clinics available in many countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Physiotherapy, mobility aids, speech therapy, cardiomyopathy management, diabetes screening, psychosocial support; referral to trials when eligible
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Variation in access to specialized ataxia clinics and long-term rehab across countries
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong diagnostic access to genetics and multidisciplinary care pathways in many EU systems
